selected_real_repository_task.task_summary: Generate a proposal for a governed validation-command runner runtime that can reduce Codex dependency for allowlisted validation command execution.
selected_real_repository_task.repository_relevance: This directly addresses CODEX-DEP-REDUCE-002 from CODEX_DEPENDENCY_REDUCTION_REPORT_V1 and supports limited production usage without granting mutation authority.

proposal_usefulness_basis: The worker generated patch proposal data.
The worker generated file proposal data.
The worker generated test proposal data.
The proposal targets a real repository dependency-reduction task.
The proposal remained replay-compatible and certification-compatible.
The proposal performed no repository mutation and no command execution.

baseline_workflow: Human asks Codex to design and implement.
Human asks Codex to run validation.
aigol_worker_workflow: Human approves external worker task package.
operator_effort_basis.reduction_rationale: The AiGOL path packages governance, worker output, validation, and replay certification into one reviewable evidence chain, reducing one operator handoff while preserving human approval.

governance_integrity_basis: Human approval remained explicit for task-package generation.
Result validation consumed the generated execution result.
Replay certification consumed the validation artifact.
No hidden repository mutation or command execution occurred.
replay_integrity_basis: Task package hash bound the worker result package.
Result package hash bound the execution result artifact.
Execution result artifact passed result validation.
remaining_limits: This completes a real development proposal, not an applied code change.
Limited production usage must remain supervised until a mutation-capable external worker is separately certified.
Operator effort reduction is action-count based, not wall-clock labor telemetry.
